entries.source: A clear front that asks one idea.
entries.value: Tó mọ́ front yẹn asks ọ̀kan èrò.

entries.source: A common trap that warns against a likely mistake.
entries.value: Wọ́pọ̀ ẹ̀gẹ̀ yẹn kìlọ̀ against ṣeé ṣe aṣiṣe.

entries.source: A concise back that completes the thought.
entries.value: Kúkúrú back yẹn parí thought.

entries.source: A hint that helps without giving everything away.
entries.value: Ìtọ́ni yẹn ń ràn lọ́wọ́ láìsí giving everything away.

entries.source: A review choice so missed cards return sooner.
entries.value: Àtúnyẹ̀wò choice so missed àwọn kaadi return sooner.

entries.source: A simple flashcard workflow
entries.value: Rọrùn ìṣàn moṣẹ́ kaadi ìrántí

entries.source: A stronger card asks for recall:
entries.value: Lágbára síi kaadi asks fún recall:

entries.source: A useful card asks one thing. It should not hide five questions inside one prompt. It should be specific enough to answer, but not so narrow that it becomes meaningless.
entries.value: Tó wúlò kaadi asks ọ̀kan thing. ó yẹ kí kì í ṣe hide márùn-ún àwọn ìbéèrè inside ọ̀kan ìbéèrè. ó yẹ kí be pato enough láti ìdáhùn, ṣùgbọ́n kì í ṣe so narrow yẹn ó di meaningless.

entries.source: A weak AI flashcard says:
entries.value: Aláìlera AI kaadi ìrántí sọ pé:

entries.source: active recall
entries.value: ìrántí tí ń ṣiṣẹ́

entries.source: Add hints without giving away the answer
entries.value: Fi kún àwọn ìtọ́ni láìsí giving away ìdáhùn

entries.source: AI can help make flashcards faster, but the learning still comes from retrieval. If the tool writes the cards and the learner never struggles to answer them, almost nothing has changed. The useful version turns messy notes into focused prompts, then makes the learner recall before revealing the answer.
entries.value: AI lè ìrànlọ́wọ́ ṣe àwọn kaadi ìrántí yára síi, ṣùgbọ́n ẹ̀kọ́ síbẹ̀ wá láti ìrántí. bí irinṣẹ́ kọ àwọn kaadi àti akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ rárá ń sapá láti ìdáhùn wọn, fẹ́rẹ̀ẹ́ ohunkóhun ní yí padà. wúlò ẹ̀dà ń yí rúdurùdu àwọn akọsilẹ̀ sí dojú kọ àwọn ìbéèrè, lẹ́yìn náà ń mú akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ rántí kí tó fi hàn ìdáhùn.

entries.source: AI can turn rough notes into cleaner retrieval prompts. It can split broad ideas into smaller cards, add examples, and identify traps a learner might miss.
entries.value: AI lè yí àkọ́kọ́ àwọn akọsilẹ̀ sí mọ́ síi àwọn ìbéèrè ìrántí. ó lè pín gbooro àwọn èrò sí kéré síi àwọn kaadi, fi kún àwọn àpẹẹrẹ, àti dámọ̀ àwọn ẹ̀gẹ̀ akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ lè fojú kọ.

entries.source: AI flashcards and active recall: how to remember more
entries.value: AI àwọn kaadi ìrántí àti ìrántí aláṣmoṣẹ́: báwo láti rántí síi

entries.source: apply a concept to a small example.
entries.value: apply èrò láti small example.

entries.source: Ask for one-idea cards with hints and common traps.
entries.value: Béèrè fún one-idea àwọn kaadi pẹ̀lú àwọn ìtọ́ni àti ẹ̀gẹ̀ tó wọ́pọ̀s.

entries.source: Back: "Spacing forces memory to reconstruct the answer after some forgetting, which strengthens later recall."
entries.value: Back: "Spacing forces ìrántí láti reconstruct ìdáhùn lẹ́yìn some gbígbàgbé, èyí wo strengthens later recall."

entries.source: compare two ideas;
entries.value: fi wé méjì àwọn èrò;

entries.source: define a term;
entries.value: define term;

entries.source: Delete cards that are too broad or obvious.
entries.value: Pa rẹ́ àwọn kaadi yẹn jẹ́ púpọ̀ gbooro tàbí obvious.

entries.source: Do not review everything equally
entries.value: Ṣe kì í ṣe àtúnyẹ̀wò everything equally

entries.source: Easy. Push it later.
entries.value: Easy. Push ó later.

entries.source: explain a cause;
entries.value: ṣàlàyé cause;

entries.source: Flashcards are one of the simplest ways to do this.
entries.value: Àwọn kaadi ìrántí jẹ́ one ti simplest ways láti ṣe yìí.

entries.source: Flashcards are powerful, but they are not the whole study process. They are best for retrieval, vocabulary, definitions, formulas, distinctions, and key examples. They are weaker when the learner has never understood the idea in the first place.
entries.value: Àwọn kaadi ìrántí jẹ́ lágbára, ṣùgbọ́n wọ́n jẹ́ kì í ṣe gbogbo ẹ̀kọ́ ṣe ilana. wọ́n jẹ́ tó dára jù fún ìrántí, ọ̀rọ̀, àwọn ìtumọ̀, àwọn fọ́ọ̀mùlà, àwọn ìyàtọ̀, àti pàtàkì àwọn àpẹẹrẹ. wọ́n jẹ́ rọ̀ síi nígbà tí akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ ní rárá lóye èrò nínú àkọ́kọ́ ibi.

entries.source: Flashcards work best when they test one idea at a time, reveal answers only after effort, and review missed cards sooner.
entries.value: Àwọn kaadi ìrántí iṣẹ́ tó dára jù nígbà tí wọ́n dánwò ọ̀kan èrò ní àkókò, reveal àwọn ìdáhùn nìkan lẹ́yìn effort, àti àtúnyẹ̀wò missed àwọn kaadi sooner.

entries.source: For a deeper route, read the Flashcard Builder guide and the Flashcard Builder prompts and study loop. The goal is not a beautiful deck. The goal is remembering more when the notes are gone.
entries.value: Fún jinlẹ̀ síi ọ̀nà, ka kaadi ìrántí Olùkọ́ ìtọ́sọ́nà àti kaadi ìrántí Olùkọ́ àwọn ìbéèrè àti ẹ̀kọ́ ìyípo. àfojúsùn jẹ́ kì í ṣe rẹwà akopọ̀. àfojúsùn jẹ́ rírántí síi nígbà tí àwọn akọsilẹ̀ jẹ́ lọ.

entries.source: For example:
entries.value: Fún example:

entries.source: Front: "Why does spaced review work better than cramming?"
entries.value: Front: "kí nìdí ṣe spaced àtúnyẹ̀wò iṣẹ́ dára síi than cramming?"

entries.source: Hard. Review after a short delay.
entries.value: Hard. àtúnyẹ̀wò lẹ́yìn short delay.

entries.source: Hint: "Think about forgetting and retrieval."
entries.value: Ìtọ́ni: "Ronú nípa gbígbàgbé àti ìrántí."

entries.source: Hints are useful when they preserve effort. A bad hint is the answer in disguise. A good hint narrows the search space.
entries.value: Àwọn ìtọ́ni jẹ́ wúlò nígbà tí wọ́n preserve ìsapá. bad ìtọ́ni jẹ́ ìdáhùn nínú disguise. dára ìtọ́ni narrows wá space.

entries.source: identify a common mistake;
entries.value: dámọ̀ wọ́pọ̀ aṣiṣe;

entries.source: If a card keeps failing, do not only repeat it. Ask why it is hard. Use Socratic Instruction to uncover the misconception, Math Step Coach for procedural gaps, or the AI learning prompt library to find a better study prompt.
entries.value: Bí kaadi ń pa failing, ṣe kì í ṣe nìkan tún ṣe ó. béèrè kí nìdí ó jẹ́ nira. lo Socratic Instruction láti ṣí payá ìmọ̀ aṣìṣe, Math Step olùkọ́ fún ti ilana àwọn àlàfo, tàbí AI ẹ̀kọ́ ìbéèrè ìkàwé láti rí dára síi ẹ̀kọ́ ìbéèrè.

entries.source: inspir's Flashcard Builder is designed around that active recall loop: build a deck, try the front, reveal the answer, rate your recall, and review the misses until the idea sticks.
entries.value: Inspir's kaadi ìrántí Olùkọ́ jẹ́ ṣe àpẹrẹ yí ká yẹn ìrántí aláṣiṣẹ́ ìyípo: kọ́ akopọ̀, gbìyànjú front, fi hàn ìdáhùn, ṣe oṣùwọ̀n tirẹ recall, àti àtúnyẹ̀wò àwọn tí o padà sí títí èrò fi mọ́ra.

entries.source: Missed. Review soon.
entries.value: Missed. àtúnyẹ̀wò soon.

entries.source: One mistake learners make is treating every card the same. If you know a card instantly, reviewing it again tomorrow may waste time. If you miss a card, waiting a week may be too long.
entries.value: One aṣiṣe àwọn akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ ṣe jẹ́ treating gbogbo kaadi kan náà. bí ìwọ mọ̀ kaadi instantly, reviewing ó again tomorrow lè waste àkókò. bí ìwọ fojú kọ kaadi, waiting week lè jẹ́ púpọ̀ long.

entries.source: Pair flashcards with understanding
entries.value: Pair àwọn kaadi ìrántí pẹ̀lú understanding

entries.source: Paste a small chunk of notes into Flashcard Builder.
entries.value: Paste small chunk ti àwọn akọsilẹ̀ sí olùkọ́ kaadi ìrántí.

entries.source: Photosynthesis: the process plants use to make food.
entries.value: Photosynthesis: ilana plants lo láti ṣe food.

entries.source: Rate recall honestly.
entries.value: Ṣe oṣùwọ̀n ìrántí rẹ ní òtítọ́.

entries.source: Reading notes feels productive. Remembering them later is the real test.
entries.value: Reading àwọn akọsilẹ̀ feels productive. Remembering wọn later jẹ́ gidi dánwò.

entries.source: remember a sequence;
entries.value: rántí sequence;

entries.source: Review without looking at the back.
entries.value: Àtúnyẹ̀wò láìsí looking ní back.

entries.source: Strong cards often include:
entries.value: Lágbára àwọn kaadi often fi kún:

entries.source: study skills
entries.value: ọgbọ́n ẹ̀kọ́

entries.source: That hint helps the learner start without removing the act of remembering. inspir's Flashcard Builder is designed for this reveal-and-rate loop: try first, reveal second, rate honestly, then bring weak cards back sooner.
entries.value: Yẹn ìtọ́ni ràn lọ́wọ́ akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ bẹ̀rẹ̀ láìsí removing act ti rírántí. inspir's kaadi ìrántí Olùkọ́ jẹ́ ṣe àpẹrẹ fún yìí reveal-and-ṣe oṣùwọ̀n ìyípo: gbìyànjú àkọ́kọ́, fi hàn second, ṣe oṣùwọ̀n honestly, lẹ́yìn náà bring aláìlera àwọn kaadi back sooner.

entries.source: That is why active recall matters. Instead of looking at the answer, you try to pull it from memory. The effort strengthens the memory and reveals what still needs work.
entries.value: Yẹn jẹ́ kí nìdí ìrántí aláṣiṣẹ́ ṣe pàtàkì. Instead ti looking ní ìdáhùn, ìwọ gbìyànjú láti pull ó láti ìrántí. ìsapá strengthens ìrántí àti reveals kí síbẹ̀ nílò iṣẹ́.

entries.source: The learner still has to do the remembering. That is the point.
entries.value: Akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ síbẹ̀ ní láti ṣe rírántí. yẹn jẹ́ point.

entries.source: The second card makes the learner retrieve a relationship, not simply recognize a sentence. Good cards usually focus on one of these jobs:
entries.value: Second kaadi ń mú akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ retrieve relationship, kì í ṣe simply recognize sentence. dára àwọn kaadi sábà focus lórí one ti wọ̀nyí àwọn iṣẹ́:

entries.source: This does not need to be complicated. The important part is that your review schedule responds to memory instead of treating the deck like a static list.
entries.value: Yìí ṣe kì í ṣe nílò láti jẹ́ complicated. important part jẹ́ yẹn tirẹ àtúnyẹ̀wò schedule responds láti ìrántí dípò ti treating akopọ̀ like static list.

entries.source: Try it in Flashcard Builder. If you need to understand the topic before making cards, start in Learn Anything. If you want pressure after reviewing cards, move into Quiz Me On Trivia or Viva Practice.
entries.value: Gbìmọ̀ wò ó nínú olùkọ́ kaadi ìrántí. bí ìwọ nílò láti lóye kókó kí mímú àwọn kaadi, bẹ̀rẹ̀ nínú kọ́ Anything. bí ìwọ fẹ́ pressure lẹ́yìn reviewing àwọn kaadi, lọ sí quiz mi lórí Trivia tàbí Viva adaṣe.

entries.source: Turn notes into better cards
entries.value: Yí àwọn akọsilẹ̀ sí dára síi àwọn kaadi

entries.source: Turn repeated misses into a question for Learn Anything.
entries.value: Yí repeated àwọn tí o padà sí sí ìbéèrè fún kọ́ Anything.

entries.source: Use this loop:
entries.value: Lo yìí ìyípo:

entries.source: Use three ratings:
entries.value: Lo mẹ́ta ratings:

entries.source: What is the role of chlorophyll in photosynthesis?
entries.value: Kí ni Jẹ́ role ti chlorophyll nínú photosynthesis?

entries.source: What makes a good flashcard
entries.value: Kí ń mú good kaadi ìrántí

entries.source: When you paste notes into AI, ask it to split broad ideas into those jobs. A deck with 18 specific cards is usually better than a deck with 5 giant cards that each contain a whole paragraph.
entries.value: Nígbà tí ìwọ lẹ́ẹ̀mọ́ àwọn akọsilẹ̀ sí AI, béèrè ó láti pín gbooro àwọn èrò sí ìwọ̀nyẹn àwọn iṣẹ́. akopọ̀ pẹ̀lú 18 pato àwọn kaadi jẹ́ sábà dára síi than akopọ̀ pẹ̀lú 5 giant àwọn kaadi yẹn kọọkan contain gbogbo paragraph.

entries.source: Where AI helps
entries.value: Ibo AI ń ràn lọ́wọ́
